quality.rationale: 基于度规相容性与正交归一条件推导联络代数性质，逻辑链条清晰且具一般性

origin.question: Problem 7.17. Consider an observer with 4-velocity $\mathbf{u}$ who transports his four basis vectors $\mathbf{e}_{\alpha}$ along with him according to the transport law $\nabla_{\mathbf{u}}\mathbf{e}_{\alpha} = \mathbf{A}_{\alpha}^{\beta}\mathbf{e}_{\beta}$ . What is the most general form of $\mathbf{A}_{\alpha}^{\beta}$ if:\n(i) the basis vectors are to be orthonormal?
origin.answer: The tensor $A_{\\alpha\\beta} \\equiv \\eta_{\\beta\\gamma}A_{\\alpha}^{\\gamma}$ must be antisymmetric: $A_{\\alpha\\beta} = -A_{\\beta\\alpha}$. Equivalently, $A_{\\alpha}^{\\beta}$ generates infinitesimal local Lorentz transformations.
origin.solution: To preserve orthonormality, the inner product of any two basis vectors must remain constant along the observer's worldline: $\\frac{d}{d\\tau} g(\\mathbf{e}_\\alpha, \\mathbf{e}_\\beta) = 0$. Using the compatibility of the Levi-Civita connection ($\\nabla g = 0$), we have $g(\\nabla_{\\mathbf{u}}\\mathbf{e}_\\alpha, \\mathbf{e}_\\beta) + g(\\mathbf{e}_\\alpha, \\nabla_{\\mathbf{u}}\\mathbf{e}_\\beta) = 0$. Substituting the transport law $\\nabla_{\\mathbf{u}}\\mathbf{e}_\\alpha = A_\\alpha^{\\gamma}\\mathbf{e}_\\gamma$ yields $g(A_\\alpha^{\\gamma}\\mathbf{e}_\\gamma, \\mathbf{e}_\\beta) + g(\\mathbf{e}_\\alpha, A_\\beta^{\\gamma}\\mathbf{e}_\\gamma) = 0$. In an orthonormal frame, $g(\\mathbf{e}_\\gamma, \\mathbf{e}_\\beta) = \\eta_{\\gamma\\beta}$, so this becomes $A_\\alpha^{\\gamma}\\eta_{\\gamma\\beta} + \\eta_{\\alpha\\gamma}A_\\beta^{\\gamma} = 0$, which simplifies to $A_{\\alpha\\beta} + A_{\\beta\\alpha} = 0$. Thus, the most general form requires $A_{\\alpha\\beta}$ to be antisymmetric in its lower indices, meaning $A_\\alpha^{\\beta}$ represents an infinitesimal Lorentz rotation/boost.
